doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’m glad to be here. So, I’ve been having this pain at the base of my left thumb for about two days now. It started off kinda achy, but it gets really sharp when I move it, especially when I’m, like, gripping things or writing. It’s been pretty uncomfortable… I’d rate it about a 6 out of 10, I guess.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your thumb?
patient_turn: Uh, it was about two days ago. I mean, it just sort of started out as this dull ache, but then it got worse, you know? Now it really hurts when I try to move my thumb or do anything with my hand.

doctor_turn: Can you show me exactly where the pain is located on your thumb?
patient_turn: Sure, um, it’s right at the base of my left thumb, like, where it connects to my hand. That’s where it hurts the most, especially when I try to move it or grip something. It’s, um, really tender there.

doctor_turn: Does the pain ever spread to any other areas, or is it just in your thumb?
patient_turn: Uh, I think it’s mostly just in my thumb, but sometimes, I feel like it might kind of go a little into my wrist? Not really sure though.

doctor_turn: How would you describe the pain in your thumb? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: Well, it’s, um, kind of both, I guess? It starts off as this achy, dull pain, but then it becomes really sharp when I move my thumb or grip something. It’s, uh, pretty uncomfortable, to be honest.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your thumb?
patient_turn: Uh, I think it started a couple of days ago, maybe two or three days? It’s a bit fuzzy in my mind, honestly.

doctor_turn: Does the pain ever spread to other areas, or is it just in your thumb?
patient_turn: Um, I think it’s mostly just in my thumb. Sometimes I feel a little bit in my wrist, but it’s really concentrated at the base of my thumb. So, yeah, it’s mainly just there.

doctor_turn: Have you ever injured your hand or thumb in the past?
patient_turn: Uh, no, I haven’t had any injuries or anything like that before. This just sort of came out of nowhere, you know? I’m kinda worried because I’ve never had anything like this happen.

doctor_turn: Have you had any past medical conditions or surgeries that we should know about?
patient_turn: Um, no, I don’t have any past medical conditions or surgeries. I’ve been pretty healthy overall, I guess. Just this thumb pain is, uh, really the only issue I’m dealing with right now.

doctor_turn: Are you currently taking any medications or supplements?
patient_turn: Uh, no, I’m not taking any medications or supplements at the moment. I haven’t really needed anything, you know? Just trying to deal with this thumb pain.

doctor_turn: Do you have any allergies to medications or anything else?
patient_turn: Um, no, I don’t have any allergies that I know of. I’ve never had a reaction to medications or anything like that, so, uh, I think I’m good there.

doctor_turn: Does anyone in your family have a history of musculoskeletal or rheumatological diseases?
patient_turn: Uh, no, not really. My family history is pretty clear of those kinds of diseases. There are some cancers, like colon and breast cancer, in second-degree relatives, but nothing related to musculoskeletal issues.

doctor_turn: What is your living situation like, and do you have any daily habits or routines?
patient_turn: Um, I live alone, and I mostly work online. It’s been a bit tough lately with this thumb pain, so I’ve stopped doing handwritten note-taking, which helped a little. I don’t smoke anymore, but I do drink occasionally on weekends and use cannabis edibles about once a month. Just trying to manage everything, you know?

doctor_turn: How has this pain affected your work or daily activities?
patient_turn: Uh, it’s been pretty challenging, to be honest. I’ve had to stop doing handwritten notes because it hurts too much, and that’s made things a bit harder for me at work. I’m kinda worried about how it’s affecting my productivity, you know?
